task_yaml.task:
I want a verified inventory of real MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers so I can pick some to try. Please research this live — search GitHub and the web — and do not rely on your prior memory, because the ecosystem changes quickly.

An MCP server is a program that exposes tools/resources/prompts to an MCP client (like Claude Desktop or an IDE) over the MCP protocol, usually via stdio or HTTP/SSE. I specifically want actual servers — not "awesome" lists of servers, not client libraries/SDKs, and not inspector/debugging tools. If something looks like a server by name but is really a list or a client, exclude it (or clearly flag it as not-a-server).

Find at least 12 genuine MCP servers. For each one, record:
- the repository or official link
- what it does (which tools/resources it exposes)
- its primary language
- an approximate star count or other popularity signal
- whether it is official/first-party or community-built
- the transport it uses (stdio / HTTP / SSE) where you can determine it

Verify each entry by actually looking at the repo (read its README/description), not just by matching the name "mcp". Be sure to include the official reference servers collection if you find it.

Save the inventory to `/tmp_workspace/results/mcp_server_landscape.md`, organized clearly, and add a short note listing any name-look-alikes you deliberately excluded and why.
